question: How many financial metrics are associated with each call report for the report date '20240630'?
gold_sparql:
SELECT ?callReportId (COUNT(?financialMetric) AS ?metricCount) FROM <https://omnix.dev/graphs/demo-tenant/kg/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports> WHERE {
  ?callReport <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/type/CallReport> .
  ?callReport <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/pred/callreport_has_metric> ?financialMetric .
  ?financialMetric <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/type/FinancialMetric> .
  ?callReport <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/pred/callreport_repdte> "20240630" .
  ?callReport <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/pred/callreport_id> ?callReportId .
} GROUP BY ?callReportId

merged_data = dfs['CallReport'].merge(dfs['Bank'], left_on='callreport_cert', right_on='bank_cert')
merged_data = merged_data.merge(dfs['BankClass'], left_on='bank_bkclass', right_on='bankclass_code')

filtered_banks = merged_data[
    (merged_data['callreport_fiscal_quarter'] == 'Q2') &
    (merged_data['callreport_fiscal_year'] == 2024) &
    (merged_data['bankclass_charter_category'].str.lower() == 'commercial')
]

result = filtered_banks['bank_name'].unique().tolist()

per_seed.code:
merged_data = dfs['CallReport'].merge(dfs['Bank'], left_on='callreport_cert', right_on='bank_cert')
merged_data = merged_data.merge(dfs['BankClass'], left_on='bank_bkclass', right_on='bankclass_code')

filtered_banks = merged_data[
    (merged_data['callreport_fiscal_quarter'] == 'Q2') &
    (merged_data['callreport_fiscal_year'] == 2024) &
    (merged_data['bankclass_charter_category'].str.lower() == 'commercial')
]

result = filtered_banks['bank_name'].unique().tolist()

per_seed.code:
import pandas as pd

# Filter CallReport for Q2 2024
call_reports_q2_2024 = dfs['CallReport'][
    (dfs['CallReport']['callreport_fiscal_year'] == 2024) & 
    (dfs['CallReport']['callreport_fiscal_quarter'] == 'Q2')
]

# Filter BankClass for 'commercial' charter category
commercial_classes = dfs['BankClass'][
    dfs['BankClass']['bankclass_charter_category'].str.lower() == 'commercial'
]['bankclass_code'].unique()

# Filter Bank for those in commercial classes
commercial_banks = dfs['Bank'][dfs['Bank']['bank_bkclass'].isin(commercial_classes)]

# Merge to find banks that meet both criteria
merged = pd.merge(
    commercial_banks, 
    call_reports_q2_2024, 
    left_on='bank_cert', 
    right_on='callreport_cert'
)

# Get distinct bank names
result = merged['bank_name'].unique().tolist()

question: For each state, how many banks headquartered there have at least one financial metric with a value greater than 1000?
gold_sparql:
SELECT ?stateName (COUNT(DISTINCT ?bank) AS ?numberOfBanks) FROM <https://omnix.dev/graphs/demo-tenant/kg/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports> WHERE {
  ?state <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/type/State> .
  ?state <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/pred/state_name> ?stateName .
  ?bank <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/type/Bank> .
  ?bank <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/pred/bank_headquartered_in_state> ?state .
  ?metric <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/type/FinancialMetric> .
  ?metric <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/pred/metric_for_bank> ?bank .
  ?metric <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/pred/metric_value> ?metricValue .
  FILTER (xsd:float(?metricValue) > 1000)
} GROUP BY ?stateName
ORDER BY DESC(?numberOfBanks)
expected_answer:
Texas | 346
Illinois | 328
Minnesota | 226
Iowa | 221
Missouri | 193
Kansas | 186
Oklahoma | 172
Ohio | 154
Wisconsin | 152
Nebraska | 139
... (55 total rows)

per_seed.code:
merged = dfs['Bank'].merge(dfs['FinancialMetric'], left_on='bank_cert', right_on='metric_cert')
filtered = merged[merged['metric_value'] > 1000]
banks_per_state = filtered.groupby('bank_stalp')['bank_cert'].nunique().reset_index()
result = banks_per_state.values.tolist()

per_seed.answer: SANDBOX___PANDAS_AGENT_ERROR__ SyntaxError unterminated string literal (detected at line 2) (<string>, line 2)
per_seed.code:
{"code": "import pandas as pd

# Filter financial metrics with value > 1000
metrics_gt_1000 = dfs['FinancialMetric'][dfs['FinancialMetric']['metric_value'] > 1000]

# Get unique bank certificates from these metrics
banks_with_high_metrics = metrics_gt_1000['metric_cert'].unique()

# Filter banks that are in the list of banks with high metrics
banks_filtered = dfs['Bank'][dfs['Bank']['bank_cert'].isin(banks_with_high_metrics)]

# Group by state and count unique banks
state_counts = banks_filtered.groupby('bank_stalp')['bank_cert'].nunique().reset_index()

# Merge with State table to ensure we have state names (though the question implies state codes are fine, usually list [state, count])
# The question asks 'For each state', so we'll return a list of [state_code, count]
result = state_counts.values.tolist()"}
()"}

merged = dfs['Bank'].merge(dfs['FinancialMetric'], left_on='bank_cert', right_on='metric_cert')
filtered = merged[merged['metric_value'] > 1000]
banks_per_state = filtered.groupby('bank_stalp')['bank_cert'].nunique().reset_index()
result = banks_per_state.values.tolist()

question: List the bank names and their primary regulators for banks that have a 'Tier 1 Risk-Based Capital Ratio' metric reported on '20240630'.
gold_sparql:
SELECT DISTINCT ?bankName ?primaryRegulator FROM <https://omnix.dev/graphs/demo-tenant/kg/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports> WHERE {
  ?bank <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/type/Bank> .
  ?bank <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/pred/bank_name> ?bankName .
  ?bank <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/pred/bank_classified_as> ?bankClass .
  ?bankClass <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/type/BankClass> .
  ?bankClass <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/pred/bankclass_primary_regulator> ?primaryRegulator .
  ?metric <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type> <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/type/FinancialMetric> .
  ?metric <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/pred/metric_for_bank> ?bank .
  ?metric <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/pred/metric_name> "Tier 1 Risk-Based Capital Ratio" .
  ?metric <https://omnix.dev/holdout-v2/holdout-v2-fdic-call-reports/pred/metric_repdte> "20240630" .
}

per_seed.code:
import pandas as pd

# Filter FinancialMetric for the specific metric and date
metrics = dfs['FinancialMetric']
filtered_metrics = metrics[(metrics['metric_name'] == 'Tier 1 Risk-Based Capital Ratio') & (metrics['metric_repdte'] == 20240630)]

# Get unique bank certificates from the filtered metrics
certs = filtered_metrics['metric_cert'].unique()

# Filter Bank table for these certificates
banks = dfs['Bank'][dfs['Bank']['bank_cert'].isin(certs)]

# Join with BankClass to get the primary regulator
bank_classes = dfs['BankClass']
merged = pd.merge(banks, bank_classes, left_on='bank_bkclass', right_on='bankclass_code')

# Select bank_name and bankclass_primary_regulator and convert to list of lists
result = merged[['bank_name', 'bankclass_primary_regulator']].values.tolist()

per_seed.code:
import pandas as pd

# Filter FinancialMetric for the specific metric and date
metrics = dfs['FinancialMetric']
filtered_metrics = metrics[(metrics['metric_name'] == 'Tier 1 Risk-Based Capital Ratio') & (metrics['metric_repdte'] == 20240630)]

# Get unique bank certificates from the filtered metrics
certs = filtered_metrics['metric_cert'].unique()

# Filter Bank table for these certificates
banks = dfs['Bank'][dfs['Bank']['bank_cert'].isin(certs)]

# Join with BankClass to get the primary regulator
bank_classes = dfs['BankClass']
merged = pd.merge(banks, bank_classes, left_on='bank_bkclass', right_on='bankclass_code')

# Select bank_name and bankclass_primary_regulator and convert to list of lists
result = merged[['bank_name', 'bankclass_primary_regulator']].values.tolist()

per_seed.code:
import pandas as pd

# Filter FinancialMetric for the specific metric and date
metrics = dfs['FinancialMetric']
filtered_metrics = metrics[(metrics['metric_name'] == 'Tier 1 Risk-Based Capital Ratio') & (metrics['metric_repdte'] == 20240630)]

# Get unique bank certificates from the filtered metrics
certs = filtered_metrics['metric_cert'].unique()

# Filter Bank table for these certificates
banks = dfs['Bank'][dfs['Bank']['bank_cert'].isin(certs)]

# Join with BankClass to get the primary regulator
bank_classes = dfs['BankClass']
merged = pd.merge(banks, bank_classes, left_on='bank_bkclass', right_on='bankclass_code')

# Select bank_name and bankclass_primary_regulator and convert to list of lists
result = merged[['bank_name', 'bankclass_primary_regulator']].values.tolist()
